meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Mastering SaaS Pricing: A Student Guide to Building Apps
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' block by building a SaaS pricing model. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first portfolio-ready app.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea. Build a robust SaaS pricing engine to solve the 'no project ideas' dilemma. By architecting subscription tiers, usage-based billing, and conversion analytics, you gain the exact technical skills employers demand. Transform abstract concepts into a tangible, revenue-ready prototype that anchors your developer portfolio.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a developer's greatest adversary. You have the skills, you know the syntax, but you’re paralyzed by the 'what.' This isn't just about code; it's about the architecture of value. When you build a pricing model for a SaaS, you aren't just writing logic—you are designing the economic heartbeat of a modern software business.

Today, we break that cycle of indecision. We will move beyond CRUD apps and dive into the mechanics of tiered pricing, Stripe integration, and usage-based throttling. This is your gateway to building a project that doesn't just work, but demonstrates that you understand the product lifecycle from the ground up.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Establish the data structure for your pricing plans. We need to handle free, pro, and enterprise tiers with distinct feature flags.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model supporting tiered subscription plans, including feature limits and billing intervals.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create pricing-schema.ts based on the generated interface'

steps.title: Implement Usage-Based Throttling
steps.description: Create a middleware layer that checks user subscription limits before executing API calls, ensuring paid features remain gated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Node.js middleware that validates user subscription status against a database record before allowing access to a restricted route.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Payment Flow
steps.description: Set up the backend logic to handle checkout sessions and webhooks to sync subscription state with your database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout session handler in Express.js that maps user IDs to specific subscription price IDs.

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI Component
steps.description: Develop a responsive React pricing table that dynamically reflects the data structure we defined in step one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Tailwind CSS pricing card component that toggles between monthly and yearly billing cycles.

steps.title: Automate Webhook Testing
steps.description: Ensure your system accurately updates user access rights when payments fail or succeed using simulated events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script to simulate Stripe webhook events and verify database state updates for subscription status.

pro_tips: Always store prices in cents as integers to avoid floating-point math errors in billing calculations.
Implement an 'idempotency key' for your Stripe API requests to prevent double-charging during network retries.
Use feature flags instead of hard-coding subscription checks to allow for dynamic plan updates without redeploying.
Cache subscription status in Redis to reduce database hits on high-traffic API routes.
Design your schema to support 'grandfathered' pricing so you can change public prices without breaking existing user contracts.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is functional, deploy your app to Vercel or Railway and share the link on your LinkedIn portfolio to show recruiters your end-to-end SaaS capability.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade pricing engine and a solid foundation for your first real-world SaaS project. Don't let this momentum fade—the industry needs builders who understand the intersection of code and commerce. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
